why: Protocol 775 Configuration serverbound minecraft:resource_pack must dispatch from the official framed packet id to a resource-pack response body decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet-support evidence only and is separate from runtime resource pack UI, accept/reject behavior, or pack download/reload behavior.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_resource_pack_response_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Serverbound, packet id 6, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration serverbound packet id 0x06.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ServerboundResourcePackPacket(UUID, Action), ServerboundResourcePackPacket.STREAM_CODEC, ConfigurationProtocols.SERVERBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ServerboundResourcePackPacket), ConfigurationProtocols.SERVERBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), ServerboundResourcePackPacket.id(), ServerboundResourcePackPacket.action(), and ServerboundResourcePackPacket.Action.isTerminal().
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration serverbound resource_pack dispatch.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration serverbound mapping contains client_information id 0x00, finish_configuration id 0x03, keep_alive id 0x04, and pong id 0x05, but not resource_pack id 0x06.

observed_failure: bad packet id 0x6 in Serverbound Configuration; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Configuration serverbound resource_pack packet id 6
